scheduler.title: Scheduler

scheduler.backup_standalone_desc: Runs on its own schedule, independent of scrapes. Uses the same options (daily, weekly, monthly, every N days, or custom cron).
scheduler.backup_desc: Scheduled backups run independently of scrapes.
scheduler.aboutDescription: The scheduler runs on the server. Make sure the container is running during the scheduled time. Notifications will be sent according to your global pipeline settings.

post_scrape.title: Post-Scrape Actions
post_scrape.ignore_pending_desc: Do not enter and delete existing from DB transactions in pending status
post_scrape.fraud_notify_help: Run fraud checks after each scrape. Requires either local rules or AI access.
post_scrape.fraud_notify_toggle: Notify when potential fraud is detected
post_scrape.custom_ai_skip_if_no_tx: Skip when there are no transactions in scope
post_scrape.custom_ai_skip_if_no_tx_desc: When enabled (default), the custom query is not sent if this run has nothing to analyze. Turn off to run the query anyway with an empty list.
post_scrape.telegram_aggregate: Combine Telegram notifications (one message per run)
post_scrape.telegram_aggregate_desc: Merges scrape status, post-scrape insights, and spending digest lines into a single Telegram message when Telegram is enabled.
post_scrape.spending_digest_help: Send a short budget pace, whale, and other alert lines to notification chats after each successful scrape (skipped if nothing changed). Same signals as the dashboard bell.
post_scrape.whale_where: Whale alerts (unusually large single expenses) appear here under Dashboard → bell alerts, and in the combined Telegram message when Spending digest after scrape is enabled above.
post_scrape.review_reminder_title: Remind to set memo or category
post_scrape.review_reminder_desc: For new transactions classified as Transfers or the default category (Other), show a dashboard banner and send alerts on the channels selected below.
post_scrape.notification_help: Choose which channels receive post-scrape messages.

errors.custom_query_required: Custom AI query is required when custom mode is enabled

fraud_settings.title: Fraud & Alerts
fraud_settings.description: Configure local and AI-based fraud detection rules, thresholds, and alerts.
fraud_settings.overview_desc: Choose how fraud is detected after each scrape and when alerts should fire.
fraud_settings.rule_outlier_amount_desc: Flag charges much higher than historical amounts for the same description.
fraud_settings.rule_new_merchant_desc: Flag first-time descriptions above the ₪ threshold, or foreign-currency / Latin-only names even when the amount is small.
fraud_settings.rule_new_merchant_non_hebrew_desc: If the description has Latin letters and no Hebrew, treat as new merchant without the ₪ minimum (ILS charges included).
fraud_settings.rule_rapid_repeats_desc: Detect multiple similar charges on the same day (typical for date-only imports) or within a short time window when times are present.
fraud_settings.rule_foreign_currency_desc: Flag large non-ILS charges that may be unexpected.
fraud_settings.thresholds_intro: Thresholds only control when a rule runs. Points are fixed per rule (see below) and add up to a 0–100 score.
fraud_settings.scores_summary: Score weights: foreign currency +20; new merchant +25 (or configurable points for Latin-only / no-Hebrew names); amount outlier +15–40; repeated similar charges +10–30. Final score is capped at 100.
fraud_settings.outlier_info_p1: We group charges by the same line text (same store or label). If a new charge is much larger than what you usually pay at that place, it counts as an unusual amount for you—not compared to the whole world, only to your own past charges there.
fraud_settings.outlier_info_p2: Min history count means how many older charges we need before we know what “normal” looks like for you. Example: you already have several small grocery bills at “Market ABC”; then a single huge bill at “Market ABC” stands out. If we almost never saw that name before, we can’t compare yet.
fraud_settings.outlier_info_p3: Sensitivity is how big a jump must be before we care. Stricter settings ignore small bumps and only react to very large surprises. Example: you often pay around ₪40 at a café; ₪45 is close enough, but ₪350 on the same line is the kind of surprise this check is for.
fraud_settings.severity_bar_help: Drag M (medium) and H (high) along the bar. Green is low severity, yellow is medium, orange to red is high. Click the bar to jump the nearest handle.
fraud_settings.severity_bar_track_aria: Severity thresholds on a 0–100 score bar
fraud_settings.severity_bar_handle_medium: Drag to set minimum score for medium severity
fraud_settings.severity_bar_handle_high: Drag to set minimum score for high severity
fraud_settings.advanced_severity_low_hint: Low cutoff is kept for compatibility; severity still uses medium and high cutoffs for labels.
fraud_settings.min_new_merchant_help: Minimum ₪ for the standard new-merchant path. New merchants in foreign currency or Latin-only descriptions bypass this floor.
fraud_settings.non_hebrew_points_help: Points when the new-merchant rule applies to a Latin-only description (no Hebrew letters). Default 35. Other new-merchant cases use +25.
fraud_settings.min_fx_amount_help: Minimum original (foreign) amount before the foreign-currency rule adds +20 points.
fraud_settings.rapid_window_help: Peers count if they are on the same calendar day (works when dates are midnight-only) or their timestamps are within this many minutes.
fraud_settings.rapid_count_help: How many similar charges (same description, amount within 5%) must appear in the same scrape batch. Peers match on the same calendar day or within a fixed minute span (default 30; not editable here).
fraud_settings.outlier_history_help: How many past transactions with the same description are required before z-score outlier detection runs.
fraud_settings.outlier_zscore_help: How many standard deviations above the historical mean counts as an outlier; higher = stricter.
fraud_settings.sev_medium_help: Minimum total score to label a finding as medium severity.
fraud_settings.sev_high_help: Minimum total score to label a finding as high severity.
fraud_settings.notify_severity_help: When “notify on issue” is on, only findings at or above this severity trigger notifications.
fraud_settings.persist_only_flagged_help: If enabled, only transactions with a score above zero are stored as fraud findings.
fraud_settings.version_help: Bumped into finding IDs; change when scoring rules change so records stay consistent.
fraud_settings.test_intro: Simulate local detection with the settings above (saved or not). No data is stored.
fraud_settings.test_history_invalid: History must be valid JSON array
fraud_settings.test_would_notify: Would notify at current “notify from severity” setting.
fraud_settings.test_would_not_notify: Would not notify at current “notify from severity” setting.

dashboard.title: Financial Command Center
dashboard.select_data: Select transaction files to view your financial dashboard
dashboard.select_hint: Choose files from the sidebar to start analyzing
dashboard.recurring_hint: Recurring items are detected from your transaction history
dashboard.transfers_excluded: {{amount}} excluded from expense totals to prevent double-counting
dashboard.transfer_explanation: These are credit card settlement payments from your bank account. The individual card transactions are already counted as expenses, so these lump-sum payments are excluded to avoid double-counting.

budget_health_msg.pace_much_faster: Spending much faster than historical average.

velocity.msg: Spending in {{category}} is ahead of your usual pace for this point in the month.

outlier.msg: {{category}} is trending well above your typical monthly level.

whale.msg_with_avg: One expense of {{amount}} is much larger than your typical {{category}} charge (~{{avgAmount}}).
whale.msg_no_avg: One expense of {{amount}} stands out in {{category}} this month.

dashboard.cc_billing_desc: Set the day of the month your credit cards are cleared. Used for more accurate projections.
dashboard.message_spending_much_faster_than_histor: Spending much faster than historical average.
dashboard.forecast_calculation: Based on current daily pace and historical baseline
dashboard.ask_ai_placeholder: Ask about your spending habits, trends or specific anomalies...
dashboard.ai_disclaimer: AI can make mistakes. Please verify important financial information.
dashboard.subscription_hint: Mark regular transactions as subscriptions in their details modal.
dashboard.no_history_details: No detailed history available for this item
dashboard.forecast_details_modal_hint: Open for transaction pace vs. monthly average and full calculation
dashboard.no_transactions: No transactions found for this calculation.
dashboard.forecast_window_desc: Months of data used to compute spending averages. Capped to your available data.
dashboard.cc_patterns_desc: Transactions flagged as CC payments. Remove to stop auto-detection.
dashboard.method_transaction_count_detail: {{remainingDays}} days left in the month. The forecast expects {{forecastTxnCount}} more transactions. At ~{{avgTxnValue}} ₪ per transaction.
dashboard.forecast_explanation: Extra variable spending projected for the next {{count}} days, beyond bills we already detected.
dashboard.forecast_how_body: Only for the current calendar month: for each variable category we use your last {{months}} month(s) of history. We estimate N (typical expense txns/month) and average debit per txn. The raw gap is (N − current txns) × avg per txn, but we cap how many extra txns we assume by the share of the month left: at most N × (days left ÷ days in month). We take the lower of the gap and that cap, then multiply by avg per txn. If you already reached N txns, we add nothing. Categories without a baseline use (spent so far ÷ days elapsed) × days left. The total is only the portion above known upcoming bills per category.
dashboard.txn_pace_modal_current: {{current}} expense transactions so far vs ~{{expected}} expected by today (pro‑rated from your long‑term average of {{avg}} transactions per month).
dashboard.txn_pace_modal_past: {{current}} expense transactions this month · historical average {{avg}} transactions per month.
dashboard.forecast_disclaimer: Estimates only; they change as new transactions arrive.
dashboard.top_insights_empty: Use the AI analyst chat to add scored insights—they appear here by importance.
dashboard.no_transactions_month: No transactions found for this month
dashboard.forecast_extra_vs_bills: Extra forecast vs. known upcoming bills
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_intro: Subscriptions are detected from transaction behavior and your manual overrides:
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_manual: Manual mark in transaction details always wins and sets the interval.
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_pattern: Auto-detection requires repeating timing with similar amounts and merchant text.
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_exclusions: Ignored/internal-transfer/installment transactions and excluded items are skipped.
dashboard.subscriptions_logic_rule_active: Old patterns are hidden when they look inactive for their billing interval.
dashboard.detectionAnalyzesHistory: Analyzes your entire transaction history to find consistent patterns:
dashboard.detectionRuleDescriptionMultiMonth: Description appears in multiple months (3 of last 4)
dashboard.detectionRuleEstimatedDate: Estimated date based on past occurrences (+/-3 days)

ai_chat.greeting: Hello! I am your AI financial analyst. I can help answer questions about your spending habits, trends, or anomalies. What would you like to know?
ai_chat.error: Sorry — I hit an error while analyzing your data. Please try again.

ai_memory.title: AI memory
ai_memory.description: Facts are long-term context the model can reuse (editable). Insights are analytical notes stored so the assistant avoids repeating the same observation.
ai_memory.retention_help: Automatically delete insights and alerts older than the number of days below (0 = keep forever). Pruning runs on save, daily, and when the server starts.
ai_memory.clear_all_confirm: Remove every item in “{{section}}”? This cannot be undone.
ai_memory.facts_help: Examples: goals, household situation, preferences. The AI may suggest new facts; you can edit or delete them here.
ai_memory.insights_help: Observations from past replies. Delete items if you want them to be allowed again in future answers.
ai_memory.alerts_help: Urgent or time-sensitive items produced by the assistant and stored here (scores are 1–100). Dismissing removes the row and prevents the same text from being saved again.

steps.drive_title: Default Drive folder for optional backup
steps.welcome_tip: Tip: use the EN / HE button in the header to switch language anytime.
steps.step_0_body:
This app runs on your computer. Your bank data stays local unless you choose to sync with Google.

We will walk you through optional settings: securing saved profiles, optional Telegram notifications, AI (Gemini), Google sign-in, and an optional Drive folder for backup. You can skip anything and finish later.
steps.step_1_body:
If you save bank profiles in the app, they are encrypted with a password you choose. This password is not sent to the cloud — it only unlocks your saved credentials on this machine.

Minimum 8 characters. You can skip and enable the lock later from the banner.
steps.step_2_body:
Optional Telegram bot notifications. Paste a bot token from @BotFather, or add it later under Configuration → Telegram along with allowed user IDs.

Skip this step if you do not use Telegram.
steps.step_3_body:
Gemini powers automatic categorization of transactions and the financial assistant chat. Without a key, those features stay off; scraping and viewing data still work.

Get a free API key from Google AI Studio and paste it below.
steps.step_4_body:
To connect Google Sheets and Google Drive for optional backup and exports, the app needs OAuth credentials from Google Cloud: a Client ID and Client Secret. You must also add the redirect URI below to your OAuth client in Google Cloud — it must match exactly.

Google Drive is used only for optional backup when you configure it. You can skip this and add Google later under Configuration.
steps.step_5_body: Optional backup: if you use a specific Google Drive folder for backups or exports, paste its folder ID here (the long part of the URL after /folders/). Leave empty to skip — you can pick a folder in the app later.
steps.step_6_body: That is it for the guided setup. If you saved API keys or OAuth settings, you may need to restart the server once for them to apply.

getting_started.resume_banner: App tour paused at step {{step}}. Resume when you are ready.
getting_started.footer_hint: Use the top tabs to switch areas anytime. The App Assistant and Help Docs in the header stay available. You can close this tour and resume from the banner below.
getting_started.rerun_confirm: Start the app tour again from the beginning?
getting_started.step_0_body:
This short tour walks you through the main workflow: save a bank profile, run your first scrape, then explore the dashboard, logs, and configuration.

Nothing here is required to continue — follow along when you are ready.

Help is always in the top-right of the header (next to the language switch): open App Assistant (blue bot icon) to ask how the app works. Next to it, Help Docs (question mark icon) opens the full bilingual guide (GUIDE.html) in a new tab.
getting_started.step_1_body:
Open the Scrape tab (we switch there for you). In the form at the top, choose your bank and sign in, or pick a saved profile if you already created one.

If app lock is enabled, unlock when prompted so the app can use saved credentials. Use the gear next to the form for advanced scraper options.
getting_started.step_2_body:
Still on Scrape: start a run with your profile. Watch live progress, then review transactions in the results area below.

After a successful run, your data feeds the dashboard and explorer.
getting_started.step_3_body:
The Dashboard tab summarizes income and spending by month, categories, subscriptions, and trends. Pick a month from the selector and drill into charts and lists.

This is the best place for a high-level picture of your finances.

For AI questions about your own transactions and trends, tap the floating indigo button at the bottom-right to open the AI Financial Analyst (set up Gemini under Configuration → AI first). That is separate from App Assistant in the header, which answers how to use the app and opens from the blue bot icon.
getting_started.step_4_body:
The Logs tab shows server, client, AI, and scrape activity. Use it to troubleshoot failed scrapes, API issues, or automation.

Switch log types with the tabs at the top and inspect entries when something goes wrong.
getting_started.step_5_body:
Configuration holds AI settings, memory, scheduler, scraper defaults, Google Sheets, Telegram, environment variables, and maintenance tools.

We open the Scraper section first — tune defaults here; other tabs cover integrations and automation.

env.title: Environment Variables
env.subtitle: Configure system-level settings stored in data/config/runtime-settings.json (next to your database; persists with the data folder / Docker volume)
env.profile_encryption_note: Saved bank credentials are encrypted with your app password (set via the strip under the header). If you still have ENCRYPTION_KEY in runtime-settings.json from an older setup, unlock once after upgrade — profiles are re-encrypted automatically and you can remove that variable.
env.restart_required_hint: Changes to these variables require a server restart to take effect.
env.confirm_restart_after_save: Settings saved. Restart server now to apply?
env.restart_in_progress: Server is restarting. Please refresh the page in a few seconds.
